best_prompt: You are a logical reasoning expert. Given a set of premises and a conclusion, your task is to determine if the conclusion necessarily follows from the premises using formal logic. Answer only with 'valid' or 'invalid' based on logical validity, not on factual truth.

features.name: explicit_instruction_clarity
features.description: Measures whether the prompt explicitly and unambiguously instructs the AI to focus solely on logical structure and not use real-world knowledge. 1 indicates clear and explicit instruction, 0 indicates vague or absent instruction. This feature causally affects performance because clarity in instruction ensures the AI adheres strictly to formal logic, avoiding errors from external knowledge interference.

features.name: step_by_step_guidance
features.description: Measures whether the prompt explicitly instructs the AI to follow a step-by-step reasoning process (e.g., identifying premises, conclusion, and applying logical rules). 1 indicates the presence of such guidance, 0 indicates its absence. This feature causally affects performance because a structured approach helps the AI systematically evaluate logical validity, reducing errors from oversight or misinterpretation.

features.name: response_format_restrictiveness
features.description: Measures whether the prompt explicitly restricts the AI to answering only with 'valid' or 'invalid' and no additional explanation. 1 indicates strict restriction, 0 indicates absence of such restriction. This feature causally affects performance because limiting the response format reduces the likelihood of the AI providing incorrect justifications or deviating from the required output.
